title: Digital Product Placements
summary: App, SaaS, and UI-first offer placements where the product interface is the proof.
whenToUse: Use when the offer is a digital product, app, SaaS workflow, dashboard, internal tool, or AI feature and the visual needs to prove value through the interface rather than generic software metaphors.
requiredInputs: workflow or job to be shown
UI, data, privacy, and brand constraints
riskGates: Do not invent real customer names, private data, or unsupported metrics.
Keep text sparse and directional unless exact UI copy is supplied.
Avoid generic abstract SaaS glow when the brief needs product proof.

families.title: App Home Proof
families.summary: A hero-style placement where the app home screen proves the main value.
families.businessGoal: Show what the product is and why the user should believe the offer
families.scene: A clean product hero composition with one large app screen, sparse supporting context, and a clear workflow state.
families.focus: Product UI as proof, not decoration
families.decision: Choose whether the app screen can carry the first impression
constraints: make the UI the visual evidence
families.avoid: generic laptop mockup, abstract gradient dashboard, invented metrics, fake customer logos

families.title: Operator Workbench
families.summary: The product shown as a working command center for an operator.
families.businessGoal: Make the user workflow feel concrete and usable
families.scene: A focused workbench with one operator-facing interface, relevant panels, and a visible next action.
families.focus: The product reducing work in a recognizable operator flow
families.decision: Choose whether the workflow reads as useful and credible
families.avoid: busy fake dashboard, sci-fi control room, unreadable microtext, real private data

families.title: Before And After Workflow
families.summary: A split workflow image that shows the product improving a visible process.
families.businessGoal: Clarify the change the product creates
families.scene: A clean before-and-after or old-way/new-way comparison with the product interface as the improved state.
families.focus: Visible workflow improvement anchored by the product UI
families.decision: Choose whether the improvement is legible without overclaiming
constraints: show contrast through structure, not aggressive claims
families.avoid: fake ROI numbers, competitor logos, dense comparison tables, abstract transformation swirls

families.title: Analytics Dashboard Proof
families.summary: A dashboard placement where structured data and decision cues prove the product value.
families.businessGoal: Show insight, visibility, or measurement as the offer proof
families.scene: A polished analytics dashboard with a few readable sections, simple placeholder charts, and a clear decision cue.
families.focus: Insight and product evidence through a believable dashboard
families.decision: Choose whether the dashboard communicates proof without invented data
constraints: avoid exact numeric claims unless supplied
families.avoid: fabricated customer results, dense unreadable charts, stock finance dashboard, fake certifications

families.title: Integration Handoff
families.summary: The product shown connecting systems or handing work between tools.
families.businessGoal: Make interoperability and workflow continuity visible
families.scene: A product interface with connected system blocks, handoff states, and a clear integration path.
families.focus: The product as the connective layer
families.decision: Choose whether the integration story is clear without relying on partner marks
constraints: use generic system labels unless official marks are supplied
families.avoid: unauthorized partner logos, tangled diagrams, fake compliance badges, abstract network background

families.title: Mobile Companion Flow
families.summary: A mobile-first placement that shows the app helping in the moment.
families.businessGoal: Show portability, immediacy, or field use
families.scene: A realistic mobile app screen in context, with one clear action and restrained environment cues.

subjectSlot.placement: phone screen in hand or on surface

families.focus: Mobile UI proving the use case
families.decision: Choose whether the product works as a mobile moment
constraints: avoid unsupported location or user data
families.avoid: generic phone wallpaper, illegible tiny UI, staged lifestyle shot with no product proof, fake notifications

families.title: AI Copilot Panel
families.summary: An AI-assist product panel shown inside a real workflow.
families.businessGoal: Make AI assistance concrete through an interface state
families.scene: A product workflow with a side panel, suggested next steps, and user-controlled approval cues.
families.focus: AI as an assistive product feature inside the workflow
families.decision: Choose whether the AI value reads as useful and operator-controlled
families.avoid: sentient robot imagery, glowing orb assistant, fake legal or financial advice, unsupported autonomy claims

families.title: Team Collaboration Canvas
families.summary: A shared workspace view where multiple roles align around the product.
families.scene: A collaborative product canvas with comments, assignments, and a visible decision state.
families.focus: The product making team alignment visible
families.decision: Choose whether collaboration is credible without looking like a generic project board
families.avoid: generic sticky-note wall, fake employee photos, unreadable dense comments, brand-inconsistent social UI

families.title: Admin Governance Console
families.summary: An admin or governance screen that proves control, security, or readiness.
families.scene: A calm admin console with settings, policy status, audit cues, and clear guardrails.
families.focus: Control and trust through product UI
families.decision: Choose whether enterprise readiness is visible without overclaiming
constraints: avoid real regulatory claims unless supplied
families.avoid: fake security seals, fear-based warning screens, real user data, excessive lock icons

families.title: Onboarding Activation Flow
families.summary: A first-run product flow that shows how quickly value appears.
families.businessGoal: Show ease of activation and early product value
families.scene: A short onboarding sequence or checklist with a meaningful first success state.

subjectSlot.placement: sequence of two or three app panels

families.decision: Choose whether the first-use story feels simple and credible
constraints: show the UI flow as proof
families.avoid: confetti-heavy onboarding, fake testimonials, unsupported time-to-value claims, tiny unreadable steps

families.title: Customer Proof Module
families.summary: A product-led proof section that pairs interface evidence with customer-safe proof cues.
families.businessGoal: Support trust with product evidence and approved proof scaffolding
families.scene: A web or sales-page module with product UI, abstract proof blocks, and a restrained customer-story area.

subjectSlot.placement: web module with UI and proof blocks

families.focus: Product proof supported by safe evidence framing
families.decision: Choose whether proof feels credible without invented endorsements
constraints: use placeholders for customer proof unless supplied
do not invent logos or quotes
families.avoid: fake named customer, fake analyst badge, overlarge quote, generic testimonial card with no product

families.title: Usage Context Split
families.summary: A split composition that shows the product UI next to the real work context it improves.
families.businessGoal: Connect product interface to the buyer's operating reality
families.scene: A balanced composition with product UI on one side and a realistic business context on the other.
families.focus: The interface tied to a concrete customer situation
families.decision: Choose whether context helps the product proof rather than distracting from it
families.avoid: stock office scene with tiny app screen, unrelated industry context, invented field data, decorative split panel
